projectDescription: An enterprise-grade Model Context Protocol server that orchestrates penetration testing tools—nmap, masscan, ffuf, nikto, and more—with LLM-native workflows, a plugin system, and multi-layer security controls.
keyFeatures: MCP-native server exposing 10+ penetration testing tools via JSON-RPC over stdio or REST
Automated multi-phase workflows (enumeration, vulnerability scanning) with adaptive coaching
Extensible plugin system supporting dynamic loading of custom tools and scripts
Multi-layer security: command allowlists, filesystem jail, path traversal protection, argument sanitization
Comprehensive audit logging in JSONL format with SARIF report output
Cross-platform support (Linux, macOS, Windows) with Docker container fallback for tool execution
OSINT and domain intelligence gathering via DuckDuckGo search APIs
Three server modes: stdio (MCP), REST API (Fastify + Swagger UI), and hybrid
Result caching, rate limiting, and OpenTelemetry integration for production deployments
publicApiSurface: nmap_scan — network discovery and port scanning
masscan_scan — high-speed large-scale port scanning
ffuf_fuzz — web directory and file fuzzing
nikto_scan — web vulnerability scanning
workflow_enum — automated enumeration workflow (scope: network|web|full, depth: light|normal|deep)
workflow_scan — multi-phase vulnerability assessment workflow
web_search — security-focused OSINT web search
ddg_search — DuckDuckGo search (web, images, videos, news)
ddg_spice — DuckDuckGo Spice APIs (DNS, weather, stocks, currency)
domain_intel — WHOIS, DNS, SSL, and subdomain intelligence
plugin_system — list/load/unload/execute custom plugins
filesystem_ops — sandboxed filesystem read/write/list/delete
redquanta://config — MCP resource: server configuration
redquanta://tools — MCP resource: available tools catalog
redquanta://security — MCP resource: security guidelines
redquanta://status — MCP resource: real-time server health
pentest_coaching — MCP prompt: personalized pentest coaching
vulnerability_analysis — MCP prompt: scan result analysis
methodology_guide — MCP prompt: step-by-step testing methodology
CLI: redquanta-mcp server|enum|scan|tools|config|doctor
